<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:山名神社天王祭舞楽]]
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:山名神社天王祭舞楽]]
{{Expand Japanese|山名神社天王祭舞楽|date=November 2025}}
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:山名神社天王祭舞楽]]


'''Yamana Shrine Tenno Festival Bugaku is a folk art''' form handed down in Iida , Morimachi, Shuchi District , Shizuoka Prefecture . It is dedicated in the dance hall on the grounds of Yamana Shrine during the festival ( Tenno Festival ) held on the weekend closest to July 14th and 15th .

== Overview ==
It is one of the Bugaku dances performed mainly by young children and handed down at temples and shrines around the country , but it has a unique feature in that it has been transformed into a dance of elegance . It consists of eight pieces: "Yatsuhatsuko," "Mikomai," "Tsuru," "Shishi," "Karyobin," "Ryu," "Praying Mantis," and "Yutenshishi." The dancers wear artificial objects such as cranes, dragons, and praying mantises on their heads, and during the "Dragon Dance," they climb up a pillar and bend over upside down, giving it a unique style that is different from orthodox Bugaku and contrasts with the Bugaku of Oguni Shrine and the Twelve-Step Bugaku of Amamiya Shrine .

On December 4, 1974 , it was selected as an intangible folk cultural property for which measures such as record keeping should be taken , and on January 14, 1982 , it was designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property as the Bugaku of Morimachi , Tōtōmi , along with the Bugaku of Oguni Shrine and the Twelve-Step Bugaku of Amanomiya Shrine .
{{translated page|ja|山名神社天王祭舞楽|version=93622023|comment=Imported full ja history}}




<!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis-->
[[ja:山名神社天王祭舞楽]]
{{wikidata link|Q11467613}}
[[Category:Festivals in Shizuoka Prefecture]]
[[Category:Gagaku]]
[[Category:Gion festivals]]
[[Category:History of Mori, Shizuoka]]
[[Category:Intangible Folk Cultural Properties that need measures such as documentation of Japan]]
[[Category:Wikidata no short description]]
